agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a strict one-Carrier-per-Shipment invariant with supervisor-gated reassignment. The product team requests an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be divided across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of cargo tracked through the system, guaranteed to have exactly one assigned Carrier at dispatch."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for physically transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold. Does not exist in the current domain model."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic decomposition of an over-weight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different Carrier without Supervisor approval."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"The only role currently authorized to reassign a Carrier after initial assignment."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment Lock","definition":"The invariant that once a Carrier is assigned, only a Supervisor can change it."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state-integrity constraint: the system guarantees single-carrier accountability per shipment at dispatch. Violation means no clear chain of custody.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in any non-draft state, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization constraint preserving data integrity. Without it, carrier assignments could be silently changed, breaking audit trails and accountability.","testable_assertion":"Any CarrierReassigned event must have an actor with Supervisor role; system rejects reassignment requests from non-Supervisor actors."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment must have exactly one Carrier assigned.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint: a shipment leaves on exactly one truck with one accountable carrier. This is the dispatch-gate invariant.","testable_assertion":"Dispatch command fails if Shipment.assignedCarrier is null or if more than one carrier is associated."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"If auto-split is introduced, each sub-shipment must independently satisfy BR-1 and BR-3 — one carrier per sub-shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Sub-shipments are shipments. The one-carrier invariant applies to every shipment entity in the system regardless of how it was created.","testable_assertion":"After a split operation, each resulting sub-shipment has exactly one assigned Carrier, and no sub-shipment shares a Carrier assignment record with its siblings unless explicitly assigned."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment exists with weight > 500 kg and an assigned Carrier","when":"The auto-split feature is triggered","then":"The system must NOT silently reassign or remove the original Carrier without Supervisor approval, per BR-2."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment is split into N sub-shipments","when":"Each sub-shipment is created","then":"Each sub-shipment has exactly one Carrier assigned (BR-1) and the parent shipment's lifecycle is explicitly closed or transitioned to a 'split' terminal state."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A Shipment with weight <= 500 kg","when":"The system evaluates it for auto-split","then":"No split occurs; the shipment proceeds with its single assigned Carrier unchanged."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A parent Shipment has a locked Carrier assignment","when":"Auto-split creates sub-shipments","then":"The original Carrier assignment lock is respected — the parent's carrier is not changed without Supervisor involvement; new sub-shipments get fresh assignments."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Shipment is exactly 500 kg","expected_behavior":"No split occurs. The threshold is 'exceeds 500 kg', meaning strictly greater than."},{"scenario":"Split produces a sub-shipment that itself exceeds 500 kg","expected_behavior":"Recursive split policy must be defined. Without it, the invariant is ambiguous — does the system split again or cap at one level?"},{"scenario":"Original carrier is already in transit when split is triggered","expected_behavior":"Split must be rejected or deferred. Cannot split a shipment that is physically in motion with a carrier."},{"scenario":"No available carriers for one of the sub-shipments","expected_behavior":"Split must not be finalized until all sub-shipments have a carrier assigned, otherwise BR-3 is violated at dispatch."},{"scenario":"Supervisor later needs to reassign a sub-shipment's carrier","expected_behavior":"Standard reassignment rules (BR-2) apply to each sub-shipment independently."},{"scenario":"Tracking and billing references on the parent shipment","expected_behavior":"Customer-facing tracking numbers, invoices, and SLAs linked to the parent must be either transferred or linked to all sub-shipments. Orphaned references break downstream processes."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Splitting shipments across multiple carriers increases transport costs (multiple pickups, less-than-truckload rates). Without clear cost allocation, invoicing to customers becomes ambiguous.","operational":"Introduces a new 'sub-shipment' aggregate that every downstream system (tracking, billing, reporting, dispatch) must understand. This is not a small feature — it fractures the core domain entity.","user":"Customers expect one tracking number per shipment. Splitting creates multiple tracking entities, which degrades the customer experience unless a parent-level tracking view is maintained.","risk":"HIGH. This feature directly contradicts two production invariants. Implementing it without resolving the contradiction risks corrupting carrier assignment integrity and audit trails.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must handle multi-carrier coordination for what was previously a single-carrier workflow. Dispatch logic must be rewritten."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their gatekeeping authority is bypassed for split-triggered assignments. Role definition is undermined."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive partial loads they did not originally agree to. Contractual and SLA implications."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Shipment identity changes mid-lifecycle. Tracking, billing, and delivery expectations are affected."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Cost allocation per sub-shipment and carrier must be defined. Current invoicing assumes one shipment = one carrier = one line item."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unambiguous contradiction between two explicit production invariants and the proposed feature. The feature as stated cannot be implemented without violating at least one existing business rule. Confidence is low because the product team has not acknowledged or addressed these conflicts.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between the auto-split feature and BR-1/BR-2 is clear and undeniable","Each sub-shipment must independently satisfy the one-carrier invariant if the split concept is adopted"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to formally relax the supervisor-reassignment rule or create an exception path","How sub-shipments relate to the parent in the aggregate hierarchy","Billing, tracking, and SLA implications of splitting"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model and its invariants are accurately described and currently enforced in production","The 500 kg threshold is a hard business rule, not a configurable preference"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature requires assigning different carriers to sub-shipments without Supervisor involvement. This directly contradicts BR-2 (only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier) and fundamentally challenges BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment). The original Shipment's carrier assignment is locked, yet the feature demands the system autonomously create new carrier assignments for derived entities.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Supervisors","Carriers","Customers","Finance"],"options":["A: Require Supervisor approval for the split operation itself, preserving BR-2 while enabling the split concept.","B: Introduce a formal 'Split Authorization' role or policy that is distinct from reassignment — auto-split is not reassignment, it is decomposition into new shipments, each getting a fresh (not reassigned) carrier.","C: Reject the feature as incompatible with the current domain model and propose an alternative (e.g., multi-carrier shipment type with a lead carrier)."],"recommendation":"Option B is the most domain-faithful path. Reframe auto-split as creating NEW shipments (not reassigning the existing one). The parent shipment transitions to a terminal 'split' state with its original carrier intact. Each sub-shipment is a new entity that independently goes through carrier assignment. This preserves BR-1, preserves BR-2 (no reassignment occurs), and introduces the split as a first-class domain event. However, this requires explicit product and domain agreement before implementation begins."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers to what is logically one shipment without Supervisor involvement.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment) and BR-2 (Supervisor-only reassignment)"},{"violation":"The feature introduces a 'sub-shipment' concept that does not exist in the current domain model and has undefined relationships with tracking, billing, and dispatch.","business_rule_broken":"BR-3 (dispatch-gate invariant) — unclear how dispatch works for a split shipment's children"}]}

This is the dispatch-gate invariant.","testable_assertion":"Dispatch command fails if Shipment.assignedCarrier is null or if more than one carrier is associated."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"If auto-split is introduced, each sub-shipment must independently satisfy BR-1 and BR-3 — one carrier per sub-shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Sub-shipments are shipments. The threshold is 'exceeds 500 kg', meaning strictly greater than."},{"scenario":"Split produces a sub-shipment that itself exceeds 500 kg","expected_behavior":"Recursive split policy must be defined. Without it, the invariant is ambiguous — does the system split again or cap at one level?"},{"scenario":"Original carrier is already in transit when split is triggered","expected_behavior":"Split must be rejected or deferred. Cannot split a shipment that is physically in motion with a carrier."},{"scenario":"No available carriers for one of the sub-shipments","expected_behavior":"Split must not be finalized until all sub-shipments have a carrier assigned, otherwise BR-3 is violated at dispatch."},{"scenario":"Supervisor later needs to reassign a sub-shipment's carrier","expected_behavior":"Standard reassignment rules (BR-2) apply to each sub-shipment independently."},{"scenario":"Tracking and billing references on the parent shipment","expected_behavior":"Customer-facing tracking numbers, invoices, and SLAs linked to the parent must be either transferred or linked to all sub-shipments. Orphaned references break downstream processes."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Splitting shipments across multiple carriers increases transport costs (multiple pickups, less-than-truckload rates). Without clear cost allocation, invoicing to customers becomes ambiguous.","operational":"Introduces a new 'sub-shipment' aggregate that every downstream system (tracking, billing, reporting, dispatch) must understand. This is not a small feature — it fractures the core domain entity.","user":"Customers expect one tracking number per shipment. Splitting creates multiple tracking entities, which degrades the customer experience unless a parent-level tracking view is maintained.","risk":"HIGH. This feature directly contradicts two production invariants. Implementing it without resolving the contradiction risks corrupting carrier assignment integrity and audit trails.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must handle multi-carrier coordination for what was previously a single-carrier workflow. Dispatch logic must be rewritten."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their gatekeeping authority is bypassed for split-triggered assignments. Role definition is undermined."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive partial loads they did not originally agree to. Contractual and SLA implications."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Shipment identity changes mid-lifecycle. Tracking, billing, and delivery expectations are affected."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Cost allocation per sub-shipment and carrier must be defined. Current invoicing assumes one shipment = one carrier = one line item."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unambiguous contradiction between two explicit production invariants and the proposed feature. The feature as stated cannot be implemented without violating at least one existing business rule. Confidence is low because the product team has not acknowledged or addressed these conflicts.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between the auto-split feature and BR-1/BR-2 is clear and undeniable","Each sub-shipment must independently satisfy the one-carrier invariant if the split concept is adopted"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to formally relax the supervisor-reassignment rule or create an exception path","How sub-shipments relate to the parent in the aggregate hierarchy","Billing, tracking, and SLA implications of splitting"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model and its invariants are accurately described and currently enforced in production","The 500 kg threshold is a hard business rule, not a configurable preference"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature requires assigning different carriers to sub-shipments without Supervisor involvement. This directly contradicts BR-2 (only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier) and fundamentally challenges BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment). The original Shipment's carrier assignment is locked, yet the feature demands the system autonomously create new carrier assignments for derived entities.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Supervisors","Carriers","Customers","Finance"],"options":["A: Require Supervisor approval for the split operation itself, preserving BR-2 while enabling the split concept.","B: Introduce a formal 'Split Authorization' role or policy that is distinct from reassignment — auto-split is not reassignment, it is decomposition into new shipments, each getting a fresh (not reassigned) carrier.","C: Reject the feature as incompatible with the current domain model and propose an alternative (e.g., multi-carrier shipment type with a lead carrier)."],"recommendation":"Option B is the most domain-faithful path. Reframe auto-split as creating NEW shipments (not reassigning the existing one). The parent shipment transitions to a terminal 'split' state with its original carrier intact. Each sub-shipment is a new entity that independently goes through carrier assignment. This preserves BR-1, preserves BR-2 (no reassignment occurs), and introduces the split as a first-class domain event. However, this requires explicit product and domain agreement before implementation begins."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers to what is logically one shipment without Supervisor involvement.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 (one Carrier per Shipment) and BR-2 (Supervisor-only reassignment)"},{"violation":"The feature introduces a 'sub-shipment' concept that does not exist in the current domain model and has undefined relationships with tracking, billing, and dispatch.","business_rule_broken":"BR-3 (dispatch-gate invariant) — unclear how dispatch works for a split shipment's children"}]}
